message.content:
Makefile ~ add another toolchain for rv64 
/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5 
allow user to switch toolchain between rv32 and rv64.

content.content:
1→# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
     2→# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
     6→#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
     7→#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
     8→#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
     9→
    10→TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
    11→PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
    12→
    13→CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    14→AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    15→LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    16→
    17→ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
    18→CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
    19→ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
    20→LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
    21→
    22→QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
    23→QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    24→
    25→SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
    26→NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
    27→
    28→# Assembly sources — single-precision
    29→ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
    30→           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
    31→           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
    32→           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
    33→           sf_fpclassify.s
    34→ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    35→
    36→# Assembly sources — double-precision
    37→S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
    38→             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
    39→             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
    40→             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
    41→             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
    42→             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
    43→S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    44→
    45→# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
    46→# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
    47→
    48→# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
    49→FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
    50→               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
    51→               modff fpclassifyf
    52→
    53→# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
    54→FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
    55→                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
    56→                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
    57→
    58→# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
    59→FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
    60→                     hypot remainder lrint lround
    61→
    62→# All functions to test
    63→FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
    64→
    65→# Test output directory
    66→TESTDIR = test
    67→
    68→# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
    69→V ?= 0
    70→ifeq ($(V),1)
    71→  Q =
    72→  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
    73→  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
    74→  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
    75→else
    76→  Q = @
    77→  MSG_AS =
    78→  MSG_CC =
    79→  MSG_LD =
    80→endif
    81→
    82→# --- Pattern rules ---
    83→
    84→%.o: %.s
    85→	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
    86→
    87→$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
    88→	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
    89→
    90→# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
    91→# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
    92→# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
    93→define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
    94→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
    95→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
    96→
    97→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
    98→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
    99→
   100→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   101→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   102→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   103→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   104→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   105→	else \
   106→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   107→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   108→	fi
   109→
   110→size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
   111→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   112→endef
   113→
   114→# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
   115→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
   116→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
   117→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   118→
   119→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   120→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   121→
   122→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   123→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   124→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   125→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   126→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   127→	else \
   128→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   129→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   130→	fi
   131→
   132→size-$(1): s_$(1).o
   133→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   134→endef
   135→
   136→# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
   137→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
   138→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   139→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   140→
   141→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   142→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   143→	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
   144→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   145→	else \
   146→		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
   147→	fi
   148→endef
   149→
   150→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
   151→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
   152→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
   153→
   154→# --- Top-level targets ---
   155→
   156→OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
   157→           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
   158→REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
   159→
   160→all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
   161→
   162→test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
   163→	@echo "Done."
   164→
   165→size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
   166→	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
   167→	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
   168→		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
   169→			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
   170→	@echo "  --------------------"
   171→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
   172→		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
   173→
   174→list:
   175→	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
   176→	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
   177→
   178→clean:
   179→	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
   180→
   181→.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
   182→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
   183→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))
   184→

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))

input.new_string:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

toolUseResult.oldString:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
toolUseResult.newString:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))

lines: # Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
+# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

content.text:
Done. Use `make XLEN=64 ...` for the RV64 toolchain, `make XLEN=32 ...` (or just `make ...`) for RV32.

The RV64 CPU is set to `andes-ax25` - verify that's correct for your QEMU build, and confirm the binary prefix `riscv64-elf-` matches what's in the toolchain's `bin/` directory.
